problem.title: Factorial Trailing Zeroes
problem.description: Given an integer n, return the number of trailing zeroes in n!. Note that n! = n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * ... * 3 * 2 * 1.
assumptions: Each trailing zero comes from a factor of 10 = 2×5. Since 2s are plentiful, count multiples of 5.

example.explanation: 5! = 120, which has 1 trailing zero. Count factors of 5 in n!.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Count Factors of 5 [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Trailing zeros in n! come from pairs of factors 2 and 5; since factors of 2 are always more plentiful, we only need to count how many times 5 divides into n!. We sum floor(n/5) + floor(n/25) + floor(n/125) + ... because multiples of 25 contribute an extra factor of 5, and so on. This repeated division by 5 runs in O(log n) time.
